contextual_guidance: Given the bearish market and lack of recent commands, consider reducing exposure to high-risk assets. Monitor the Fed's actions and the strength of the DXY for potential market shifts.
grok_counter: The strongest counter-argument this week is the potential for a surprise uptick in institutional adoption, as recent reports suggest major financial firms are quietly accumulating BTC amid regulatory clarity. The crowd might be missing subtle signals of ETF approvals or macroeconomic shifts favoring risk assets, which could flip sentiment rapidly.

typical_investor_behavior: Forced deleveraging and panic selling into support zones
Flight-to-safety toward cash, treasuries, and defensive factors
News-reactive overtrading and short-horizon decision compression
capitalization_playbook: Scale entries in tranches at pre-defined support; never all-in on first dip
Prioritize quality/liquidity assets and cut weak-conviction tails
Use volatility spikes to sell premium or hedge beta when confluence permits

typical_investor_behavior: FOMO chasing, leverage expansion, and momentum crowding
Narrative extrapolation (assuming recent gains persist indefinitely)
capitalization_playbook: Trim winners into strength and rotate to asymmetric setups
Tighten invalidations and reduce leverage as crowding increases
Favor relative-strength leaders, avoid late low-quality breakouts

investor_behavior_intelligence.current_behavioral_edge: Behavioral edge favors patient accumulation + risk-defined hedges while crowd panics.

drivers: Macro pressure from DXY and US10Y

prescriptive.primary_action: Low conviction: preserve optionality, focus on prep and watchlists.
prescriptive.position_sizing_rule: Scale 25/25/50 only after confluence gate and invalidation clarity.
risk_controls: No all-in entries during crisis/euphoria transitions
Cut exposure if macro cascade triggers two or more stress nodes
Prefer liquid instruments when volatility regime is elevated

market_pattern_library.investor_implication: Favor defense/cash, reduce leverage, demand stronger setup quality.

market_effects: Sharp downside wick and volatility spike
Dispersion between quality and low-quality rebounds
market_pattern_library.investor_implication: Use staged entries and prioritize quality assets over speculative laggards.
Harvest gains into strength and keep tight invalidation discipline.

macro_cascade_map.portfolio_impact: Increase defense/cash, reduce duration risk and high-beta exposure.
Prefer energy/defensive quality, avoid weak balance-sheet cyclicals.
Hedge beta, prioritize liquidity, and wait for stress normalization before re-risking.

protective_playbook: Pre-define kill-switch levels for beta and leverage before event day
Raise cash buffer and reduce crowded high-beta exposures
Use hedges (index puts / inverse beta / vol overlays) when confluence turns risk-off
Prioritize liquidity-first exits over perfect pricing in shock windows
capitalization_playbook: Stage buy ladders into dislocation only after volatility starts normalizing
Rotate into relative-strength defensive leaders first, then cyclicals on confirmation
Exploit forced-liquidation overshoots with strict invalidation and position caps
Harvest mean-reversion gains quickly while uncertainty remains elevated

risk_controls.mandatory_confirmation: Require confluence gate + stabilization signal before adding full risk

design_principles: Automate downside protection first, upside capture second
Use deterministic triggers with human override
Rate-limit execution to avoid overreaction during microstructure noise

bots.name: Volatility Circuit Breaker Bot
bots.trigger: VIX proxy spike + risk-off confluence + breadth deterioration
actions: Raise cash target to pre-set defensive threshold
Send priority alert with executed hedge summary

bots.name: Liquidity Stress Sentinel
bots.trigger: USD surge + yield spike + widening cross-asset drawdown
actions: Escalate to war-room mode with cascade diagnostics

bots.name: Dislocation Opportunity Bot
actions: Deploy staged buy ladder into predefined zones
Auto-log expected RR and risk budget before each tranche

typical_market_response: Initial risk-off gap and volatility spike
Broad index drawdown followed by policy-driven stabilization
Rotation from defensives to cyclicals/risk assets
Improved confidence and cross-border flow normalization

war_start_investing_playbook: Reduce leverage and tighten liquidity management immediately
Overweight resilient sectors (defense, energy, critical infrastructure) selectively
Avoid narrative-chasing; demand confirmation after initial shock
Keep dry powder for dislocation entries once volatility regime stabilizes
war_ending_investing_playbook: Gradually rotate from defensives into cyclical/recovery beneficiaries
Increase risk only as breadth and macro confirmations improve
Watch for inflation-growth rebalancing before full beta expansion
Capture re-rating opportunities while maintaining staggered exits
war_market_history_playbook.execution_rule: Treat war headlines as regime events: update scenario tree before placing discretionary risk.

cycle_layers.industry_rotation: Defensive/quality leadership when dollar+risk pressure rise; cyclicals on de-escalation
cycle_layers.commodity_cycle: Energy/inflation shocks drive hard-asset outperformance; disinflation favors growth duration
cycle_layers.individual_stock_cycle: Quality balance sheets outperform in stress; high-beta recoveries dominate post-shock rebounds

cycle_signals_to_track: Liquidity (policy stance, real yields, credit conditions)

loukas_style: Map higher-timeframe cycle phase first, then execute lower-timeframe entries
Treat cycle lows/highs as probability zones, not exact timestamps
Use trend + momentum confirmation around expected turning windows
cowen_style: Favor risk-managed accumulation over emotional peak-chasing
Use valuation/risk bands and macro context to scale exposure
Focus on downside management during cycle uncertainty

buffett_style_principles: Prefer durable moats, cash-flow quality, and strong capital allocation
Buy with margin-of-safety discipline instead of narrative momentum
Concentrate in highest-conviction quality when valuation allows
long_horizon_quality_framework.application_to_current_stack: Use quality filter + valuation discipline for core holdings, tactical overlays for cycle pivots.

typical_401k_behavior.edge: Track crowd lag: strongest opportunities often appear before default retirement flows rotate back in.

patterns: Quarter-end window dressing and benchmark hugging
Liquidity preference shifts during macro uncertainty
investment_firm_behavior.edge: Exploit forced positioning windows when flows become mechanical rather than thesis-driven.

macro_micro_economic_engine.decision_link: Macro sets regime constraints; micro timing selects entries/exits inside that regime.

historical_principles: Trend persistence until clear structure break
Volume/volatility expansion validates breakouts and breakdowns
Failed breakouts often mean-revert sharply in crowded regimes
technical_charting_mastery.near_term_structural_read: Mixed structure; prioritize trigger-based execution over prediction.
technical_charting_mastery.execution_protocol: Require structure break + confluence + invalidation before size escalation.
